selected_important_turn.text: How about two water, 1 firewood and 1 food, since we both value water the most?
selected_important_turn.why_important: This counteroffer is the pivotal move that shifts the exchange away from the opponent's initially more competitive claim toward a compromise. It directly shapes the allocation and frames water as the shared top priority — it therefore determines whether the talk becomes integrative or stays zero-sum.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: While cooperative, the counteroffer is somewhat generic and misses an explicit trade rationale tied to the opponent's revealed preferences (they said they have enough food and emphasized a baby/washing need). It doesn't ask whether the opponent would prefer more firewood or less food, nor does it propose a trade contingency that could increase joint value or signal stronger empathy for the opponent's urgency. That leaves potential gains on the table (creative trades or clarified priorities) and a small risk of ambiguity about whether the split addresses the opponent's real needs.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Make the offer more integrative and empathy-based: acknowledge their urgent water need, explicitly trade on items they value less (confirm they truly prefer firewood over food), or propose a package that makes a clear tradeoff (e.g., 'I can keep water if you take more firewood instead of food' or ask 'Would you prefer an extra firewood instead of food?'). Prompt a short explanation of what they'd give up to secure water so you can explore mutually beneficial swaps.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase — by opening concrete trade options tied to their stated needs you can find packages that better match each party's values.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: improve — explicitly tying concessions to the other's stated low-priority items increases perceived reasonableness and legitimacy of the split.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — clearer, empathy-grounded trade proposals reduce back-and-forth and speed convergence on a mutually acceptable bundle.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve — acknowledging urgency and offering tailored trade options builds rapport and trust.

value2reason.Low: I'm planning to go fishing and gather berries in the woords.
value2reason.Medium: I want to make sure there's plenty of time to cook stew over the fire.
value2reason.High: I need additional water because I overheat easily and it would help me cool down.

value2reason.Low: To add on what I have to feed the children.
value2reason.Medium: I want to sit besides fire the whole night
value2reason.High: T o help me cook as I came with many children

dialogue.text: Hi. I'm most interested in water. What's your highest priority?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I really need water too. I have a young baby and will do a lot of washing.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Okay. I'm not especially worried about food. What's your lowest priority?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I have enough food too. Can I take 2 water, 1 firewood and 2 food?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: How about two water, 1 firewood and 1 food, since we both value water the most?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I can offer 2 water, 1 firewood, and 1 food. It’s fair since we both value water most, and it gives you some firewood for the night.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I can take 2 water, 1 firewood, and 1 food. That leaves you with 1 water, 2 firewood, and 2 food. Would you prefer more firewood or less food to balance the trade?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I’m okay with that split. Let’s confirm: you get 1 water, 2 firewood, and 2 food, while I take 2 water, 1 firewood, and 1 food. That should work for both of us.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I think that’s a great split! Let me just double-check: you take 2 water, 1 firewood, 1 food; I take 1 water, 2 firewood, 2 food. Yep, that covers all packages. Thanks for working together!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Deal confirmed. Thanks for the collaboration!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
